candidates.name: AutoSchemaKG (dynamic schema induction + extraction)

paper.title: AutoSchemaKG: Autonomous Knowledge Graph Construction through Dynamic Schema Induction from Web-Scale Corpora

est_difficulty.rationale: simultaneously induces schema AND extracts triples bottom-up; web-scale oriented but method runs on small input; public code

candidates.distinct_from_existing: schema is INDUCED from data with zero predefined schema (vs CQ/schema-first methods); distinct schema-bottom-up paradigm

candidates.name: GPTKB (materialize KB from LLM parametric knowledge)

paper.title: GPTKB: Building Very Large Knowledge Bases from Language Models

est_difficulty.rationale: recursive expansion from seed entities querying the LLM's own knowledge (no input corpus); public code

candidates.distinct_from_existing: no input corpus — extracts KB from the model itself by recursive entity expansion; unique input modality

candidates.name: Zero-shot triple extraction from engineering standards

paper.title: LLM-based Zero-shot Triple Extraction for Automated Ontology Generation from Software Engineering Standards

est_difficulty.rationale: single zero-shot prompt → RDF triples from standards text; low machinery

candidates.distinct_from_existing: directly targets engineering/standards documents (on-topic for SPMM/STEP); simplest zero-shot baseline over standards

candidates.name: Elenchus (prover-skeptic dialogue KB engineering)

paper.title: Elenchus: prover-skeptic dialogue for knowledge base construction

est_difficulty.rationale: two-role dialogue (prover vs skeptic) to converge on coherent KB; verification-heavy

candidates.distinct_from_existing: adversarial prover-skeptic dialogue as the construction loop (distinct from single self-critique in Ontogenia)
